summary: Shimmering Here Comes Sunshine, relaxed Terrapin Station, solid drumming
blurb: The set features a bright Here Comes Sunshine and an understated Terrapin Station with Phil’s relaxed bass. Jerry delivers sweet‑sounding guitar tones throughout, while the Drums > Space segment hits the mark. Recording quality is excellent, making this a solid pick for fans of late‑'92 shows.
review:
On December 13, 1992 at the Oakland Coliseum the Dead rolled out a concise but satisfying two‑set show that feels like a hidden gem from the tail end of ’92. The first set moves through familiar terrain with tight rhythm and Jerry’s sweet‑sounding guitar tone lighting up songs like Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo and Tennessee Jed. The second set opens with a shimmering Here Comes Sunshine, followed by a relaxed Terrapin Station that lets Phil’s bass breathe – the “Terrapin Phil” vibe many reviewers praised. The Drums > Space segue is spot‑on, delivering the rhythmic drive that carries into an expansive The Other One and a poignant Morning Dew, while the encore The Weight rounds things out nicely. Across three available recordings the mix is consistently described as excellent or very good, with clear vocals and balanced instruments, so listeners can enjoy both the subtle nuances of the relaxed moments and the high‑energy peaks without distraction.

Overall this show may not be a headline‑making classic, but its tight playing, pleasant set flow, and top‑tier soundboard capture make it an essential listen for anyone tracking the band’s late‑1992 period. It showcases Jerry’s melodic phrasing, Phil’s laid‑back bass feel, and a drum duo that locks in perfectly on the space‑filled interlude – all wrapped in a recording quality that lets every detail shine.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked as positive, repeatedly praising the set’s tightness, sweet‑sounding guitar tone, relaxed Terrapin Station, and spot‑on Drums > Space segment. The consistent enthusiasm across sources warrants a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent‑sounding mix with clear instruments; highlights Here Comes Sunshine and relaxed Terrapin Station, making it the most enjoyable capture.

key_highlights: Relaxed, pleasant rendition of “Terrapin Station” (Terrapin Phil)

band_performance.Jerry: Jerry’s guitar tone is sweet and melodic throughout, adding a warm glow to every song.
band_performance.Phil: Phil delivers a relaxed, pleasant bass line on Terrapin Station, giving the set a laid‑back feel.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey (with Bill) provides a tight, spot‑on Drums > Space segment that drives the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Billy’s drumming locks in perfectly with Mickey, especially during the highlighted percussion interlude.
